category_title: Students with MLD using atypical multiples-list strategies for multiplication
minimal_context: Emily and her tutor are working on a missing operand multiplication problem during a one-on-one weekly tutoring session.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's look at this problem. What times 2 is 16?

STUDENT: You can’t do it… cause 2 doesn’t go into 16, evenly.

TUTOR: Take your time and try to work it out.

STUDENT: Yeah. (Raises one finger) 5, (raises another finger) 10.

TUTOR: Keep going.

STUDENT: (Continues tracking on fingers) 6, 12. 7, 14. Oh! 8. Duh!

options.A: Help the student more effectively employ her atypical strategy during the one-on-one tutoring sessions.
options.B: Maintain a ratio of praise to correction at 3:1 or 4:1 during the one-on-one tutoring sessions.
options.C: Implement a 'time-on/time-off' schedule during the one-on-one tutoring sessions (e.g., two minutes of work, one minute of break).
options.D: Set a timer to sound every minute, prompting the student to record whether she had positive interactions on a sheet of paper.
